questions.q: Whose son was Ashwatthama?
options: Drona's
Kripa's
Bhishma's
Shalya's
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama was the only son of Drona, the weapons master of the Kuru princes.

questions.q: What did Ashwatthama bear in his forehead from birth?
options: A third eye that opened in rage
A mark shaped like a serpent
A protective gem
A streak of white flame
questions.explanation: He was born with a gem in his brow that guarded him from weapons, hunger, and fear.

questions.q: How did Ashwatthama come by his name?
options: He was born in a stable at dawn
His birth cry sounded like a horse's neigh
He tamed Drona's warhorse as a child
He was as swift as the horses of Indra
questions.explanation: His cry at birth was like the neighing of a horse, and so he was named Ashwatthama.

questions.q: Who cursed Ashwatthama to wander the earth for three thousand years?
options: Vyasa
Draupadi
Gandhari
Krishna
questions.explanation: Krishna cursed him to roam alone, wounded and shunned, for three thousand years after he attacked an unborn child.

questions.q: What did Ashwatthama attack on the night after Duryodhana fell?
options: The city of Indraprastha
The Panchala capital Kampilya
The sleeping Pandava camp
Krishna's tent at Upaplavya
questions.explanation: He led a night raid on the Pandava camp and butchered its sleeping warriors.

questions.q: At whom did Ashwatthama finally aim Brahma's weapon?
options: The unborn child in Uttara's womb
The five Pandava brothers at prayer
Krishna's chariot and its horses
The assembled Panchala women
questions.explanation: Unable to recall the weapon, he bent it into Uttara's womb to end the Pandava line.

questions.q: Who was Ashwatthama's mother?
options: Sudeshna
Ambalika
Devaki
Kripi
questions.explanation: Drona married Kripi, the twin sister of Kripacharya, and Ashwatthama was their son.

questions.q: Which uncle joined Ashwatthama in the night raid?
options: Shakuni
Kripacharya
Shalya
Vidura
questions.explanation: His maternal uncle Kripacharya guarded the camp gates during the slaughter.

questions.q: Among which beings is Ashwatthama traditionally counted?
options: The eight Vasus
The seven Chiranjivis, the deathless ones
The eleven Rudras of heaven
The twelve Adityas of the sun
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama is named among the seven Chiranjivis, beings who live on through the ages.

questions.q: Whose death was Ashwatthama avenging in the night raid?
options: Duryodhana's
Karna's
Jayadratha's
His father Drona's
questions.explanation: He sought vengeance on the Panchalas for the killing of his father Drona, slain after laying down his arms.

questions.q: In the famous tale of Drona's poverty, what did young Ashwatthama drink believing it was milk?
options: Flour stirred into water
Rice water sweetened with jaggery
Coconut water from a temple offering
Thin curd begged from the neighbors
questions.explanation: Too poor to buy milk, Drona's household gave the boy flour mixed in water, a humiliation that drove Drona to seek Drupada.

questions.q: Who was the third warrior of the night raid, holding the gates alongside Kripa?
options: Shakuni
Susharma
Kritavarma
Vrishasena
questions.explanation: Kritavarma of the Yadavas stood with Kripa at the camp gates while Ashwatthama killed within.

questions.q: What sight gave Ashwatthama the idea for the night raid?
options: Wolves circling a sleeping herd
A cobra striking a resting mongoose
Moths burning themselves in a campfire
An owl slaughtering sleeping crows
questions.explanation: Watching an owl massacre crows asleep in a banyan tree, he resolved to strike the Pandava camp by night.

questions.q: Who barred Ashwatthama's way at the threshold of the Pandava camp?
options: The ghost of his father Drona
A dreadful figure of Shiva
The goddess Kali in shadow form
A wall of divine fire
questions.explanation: A vast and terrible being of Shiva blocked the gate until Ashwatthama won the god's favor.

questions.q: How did Ashwatthama kill Dhrishtadyumna?
options: With his father's own bow
By burning his tent around him
By beating and strangling him in his sleep, denying him a warrior's death
With the sword that Shiva had granted
questions.explanation: He woke Drona's killer with kicks and throttled him like an animal, refusing him death by a blade.

questions.q: Which five young warriors did Ashwatthama slay in the sleeping camp?
options: The five sons of Draupadi
The five grandsons of Virata
The five Kekaya princes
The five sons of Dhrishtadyumna
questions.explanation: The Upapandavas, Draupadi's five sons, died in the raid; some retellings say he mistook them for the Pandavas themselves.

questions.q: Why could Ashwatthama not withdraw the Brahma weapon when the sages commanded it?
options: He had never mastered the art of recalling it
Recalling it required a brahmin's purity he had lost
The mantra needed his father's living voice
Krishna had sealed the mantra's syllables
questions.explanation: Arjuna could retract his weapon, but Ashwatthama lacked the knowledge to recall his own and could only redirect it.

questions.q: What happened to the child struck in Uttara's womb by Ashwatthama's weapon?
options: He was born marked with a scar he bore for life
He was hidden by the gods until the danger passed
He was stillborn and revived by Krishna
He was untouched, shielded by Subhadra's prayers
questions.explanation: Parikshit was delivered lifeless, and Krishna restored him by an act of truth, saving the Kuru line.

questions.q: What did Draupadi demand as the price of Ashwatthama's life?
options: His exile beyond the sea
His father's bow and quiver
A vow that he never bear arms again
The gem set in his forehead
questions.explanation: Draupadi asked for the forehead gem rather than his death, and Bhima brought it back to her.

questions.q: Where did Ashwatthama's surrendered gem finally rest?
options: In the treasury of Hastinapura
On Yudhishthira's head
In the Ganga's deepest pool
On the hilt of Bhima's mace
questions.explanation: At Draupadi's word the gem was set upon Yudhishthira's head, a trophy of the avenged dead.

questions.q: What was Duryodhana's last act of command as he lay dying by the lake?
options: Sending word of surrender to Yudhishthira
Ordering the burning of the Kaurava camp
Anointing Ashwatthama as the army's final commander
Dispatching a last letter to Dhritarashtra
questions.explanation: The broken Duryodhana consecrated Ashwatthama as his last commander, and the night raid followed.

questions.q: What torment did Krishna's curse fix upon Ashwatthama's wanderings?
options: Wounds that never heal, reeking of pus and blood, shunned by all
Blindness by day and sight only at night
A hunger that no food could ever quiet
The voices of the slain forever in his ears
questions.explanation: Krishna doomed him to three thousand years of friendless wandering, his body stinking of pus and blood.

questions.q: Of which god's portion is Ashwatthama said to have been born?
options: Yama, lord of death
Rudra, the howling destroyer
Agni, the devouring flame
Vayu, the wild wind
questions.explanation: The epic describes him as born of portions of Mahadeva (Rudra) together with Antaka, Krodha, and Kama.

questions.q: How did Ashwatthama win past the dreadful figure of Shiva at the camp gate?
options: He recited his father's secret mantra
He promised the god the blood of the slain
He fought the figure until dawn approached
He offered his own body as an oblation into fire, and Shiva entered him with a sword
questions.explanation: When his weapons failed, Ashwatthama offered himself into a sacrificial fire, and the pleased god gave him a sword and possessed him.

questions.q: During the fourteenth night's fighting, which son of Ghatotkacha fell to Ashwatthama?
options: Alambusha
Alayudha
Meghavarna
Anjanaparva
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama slew the rakshasa Anjanaparva, Ghatotkacha's son, during the great night battle.

questions.q: What warning accompanied Drona's teaching of the Brahmashira weapon to his son?
options: That it would shorten his life with each recital
That it must never be cast at human foes
That it would obey only a lifelong celibate
That it could be summoned just once in a lifetime
questions.explanation: Drona taught the weapon reluctantly, warning Ashwatthama never to use it against human beings.

questions.q: Why did Draupadi spare Ashwatthama's life after the raid that killed her sons?
options: He was a brahmin and her husbands' guru's son, and she would not make his mother weep as she wept
He swore an oath of lifelong penance at her feet
Krishna pleaded that the curse was punishment enough
Bhima had already taken his weapons and his pride
questions.explanation: Draupadi held that killing the guru's brahmin son would only pass her own grief to the innocent Kripi.

questions.q: Where did the final weapon-duel between Arjuna and Ashwatthama take place?
options: On the empty field of Kurukshetra
On the banks of the Ganga at Hastinapura
Near the hermitage of Vyasa, where the sages stood between them
At the gates of the burning Kaurava camp
questions.explanation: Both loosed Brahma's weapon near Vyasa's retreat, and Vyasa and Narada rose between the fires to command withdrawal.

questions.q: After surrendering the gem, where did Ashwatthama go?
options: Back to Hastinapura to serve the blind king
Into the forest in the company of Vyasa
To the Himalayas to worship Shiva
Across the sea beyond the reach of men
questions.explanation: Stripped of his gem and bearing Krishna's curse, the dejected Ashwatthama departed for the forest with Vyasa.

questions.q: What name-link connects Ashwatthama to the manner of his father's death?
options: A horse named Ashwatthama drew Drona's chariot
His name was the answer to the Yaksha's final riddle
Drona had once cursed the name in a fit of anger
An elephant called Ashwatthama was slain so Drona would believe his son dead
questions.explanation: Bhima killed an elephant named Ashwatthama, and the half-true report of the death broke Drona's will to fight.
